articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.

images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

article.headline: Record-breaking Leverkusen beat Mainz to go top in Bundesliga
article.description: Bayer Leverkusen cruised past hapless hosts Mainz 05 3-0 on Saturday to record their best-ever start to a Bundesliga season and take over top spot after six matchdays.
article.linkText: Record-breaking Leverkusen beat Mainz to go top in Bundesliga

images.name: Jonas Hofmann goal 65th minute Mainz 0-3 Bayer Leverkusen
images.caption: Jonas Hofmann goal 65th minute Mainz 0-3 Bayer Leverkusen

video.caption: Jonas Hofmann goal 65th minute Mainz 0-3 Bayer Leverkusen
video.title: Jonas Hofmann goal 65th minute Mainz 0-3 Bayer Leverkusen
video.description: Jonas Hofmann goal 65th minute Mainz 0-3 Bayer Leverkusen

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (Jonas Hofmann goal 65th minute Mainz 0-3 Bayer Leverkusen) 2023/09/30 ESHEET

article.story: MAINZ, Germany, Sept 30 (Reuters) - Bayer Leverkusen cruised past hapless hosts Mainz 05 3-0 on Saturday to record their best-ever start to a Bundesliga season and take over top spot after six matchdays. Xabi Alonso's unbeaten team have now won five of their six games for 16 points, more than ever before at this stage of a Bundesliga season. VfB Stuttgart are a point behind in second place following their 2-0 win at Cologne. Bayern Munich, in fourth on 13, take on RB Leipzig later on Saturday. Mainz defender Sepp van den Berg put the visitors in front in the 18th minute when he slid in to intercept a cutback but instead pushed the ball into his own net. There was nothing lucky about Alejandro Grimaldo's 59th-minute free kick which he whipped over the wall and into the top corner. With Leverkusen keeping up the pressure, it was Germany international Jonas Hofmann six minutes later who thumped in their third goal, leaving Mainz firmly anchored in last place with just one point so far. (Reporting by Karolos Grohmann; editing by Clare Fallon)

Granit Xhaka (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Own Goal by Sepp van den Berg, 1. FSV Mainz 05.  1. FSV Mainz 05 0, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 1.
keyEvents.shortText: Sepp van den Berg Own Goal

keyEvents.text: First Half ends, 1. FSV Mainz 05 0, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 1.
Edimilson Fernandes (1. FSV Mainz 05) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  1. FSV Mainz 05 0, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2. Álex Grimaldo (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) from a free kick with a left footed shot to the top left corner.
